subject: DevOps
title: Pipeline Notifications
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Pipeline Notifications.

hook.text: The build failed 20 minutes ago. Nobody noticed because the notification went to an email nobody reads.

buildup.text: Pipeline notifications must go where the team actually looks: Slack, Teams, or the pull request itself.

discovery.text: Status checks on pull requests are the most effective. A red X blocks the merge button visually.

twist.text: Too many notifications cause alert fatigue. Notify on failure and recovery, not on every green build.

climax.text: The best teams treat a failed pipeline notification like a fire alarm — someone responds within minutes.

punchline.text: Notify where people look. Failed builds demand fast response.

quiz.question: Where are pipeline notifications most effective?
options: In Slack/Teams channels and on pull request status checks
On a physical dashboard in the CEO's office
